explanation: Windows shutdown.exe accepts hyphens (-) in addition to forward slashes (/) for command line switches. Since the rule explicitly looks for the exact strings '/r ' or '/s ', using '-r' bypasses the CommandLine condition.
The Sigma rule checks for '/s ' with a trailing space. Executing the command with the switch at the very end of the string (without a trailing space) will fail to match the condition.
Similar to the trailing space evasion, concatenating multiple switches together without spaces prevents the specific '/r ' string from being matched in the CommandLine arguments.

Bypasses the rule entirely by using PowerShell's native cmdlet to initiate a shutdown. This uses underlying Windows APIs (WMI/CIM) and does not spawn the 'shutdown.exe' process, failing the Image condition.
